revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:稲荷塚古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|image=File:Inarizuka-kofun 20111227.jpg|caption=kofun |coordinates={{Coord|35.6386|N|139.434722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |name=Inarizuka Kofun |location={{ill|Q28682528|qid=Q28682528|1=Q28682528}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |built=Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} |map_size=Total length: 38 m, Height: 4 m, Surrounding moat width: 2 m |Interred_individual= |burial goods= |Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Historic Site) |architectural_styles={{ill|octagonal kofun|qid=Q11391751|1=Octagonal Kofun}} }}
'''Inarizuka Kofun''' (稲荷塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Momogusa|ja|百草|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momogusa|1=Hyaku-sō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11580850}}, {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|qid=Q213263|lt=Tama City|1=Tama, Tokyo}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|qid=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}. Constructed in the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} during the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, it is designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Historic Site).
== Overview == During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the temple {{ill|Shifukuin|ja|資福院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shifukuin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was located east of Inarizuka Kofun. However, in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} decree and the accompanying {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} movement led to the establishment of the {{ill|Koiji Inari Shrine|ja|恋路稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koiji Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579229|1=Koiji Inari Shrine}} atop the mound. This significantly altered the structure, including the removal of the upper portion and the ceiling stones of the burial chamber. It is believed that the chamber was looted, and many artifacts were taken.
In {{ill|1952|en|1952|qid=Q5272|lt=Showa 27|1=1952}}, a teacher from Tama Municipal Mi11le School spearheaded an excavation with assistance from a {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} technician and students. The excavation revealed the tomb's preserved mound and burial chamber, confirming its multi-chamber structure. This led to its designation as a {{ill|Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1953.
Originally classified as a circular mound, re-investigation from the 1980s to the early 1990s identified the tomb as an octagonal kofun, a form previously unconfirmed in {{ill|Eastern Japan|ja|東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Japan|qq=}}.
=== Tomb Structure === Inarizuka Kofun has a total length of 38 meters, a surrounding moat 2 meters wide, and a two-tiered mound. While originally 4 meters tall, the mound was reduced to half its height during shrine construction.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} measuring 7.7 meters in total length: * '''Entrance Passage (Senshitsu):''' 1.6 meters long, 1.2 meters wide * '''Anterior Chamber:''' 2.3 meters long, 1.7 meters wide * '''Main Chamber (Genshitsu):''' 3.8 meters long, 3 meters wide
The chamber walls, constructed from cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=tuff|1=Tuff}}, include a single large stone forming the rear wall of the main chamber, measuring 1.6 meters tall and 1.2 meters wide. The chambers are built using a convex-curved structure resembling a womb, known as the {{ill|Do-bari multi-chambered design|ja|胴張複室構造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Do-bari multi-chambered design|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This sophisticated design is attributed to technology introduced by immigrants from the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Korean Peninsula|qid=Q483134}}.
The floor was lined with red powder, known as {{ill|Bengala|ja|弁柄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bengara|qq=}} (iron oxide), and scattered with cobblestones as paving.
== Historical Significance == Octagonal kofun were traditionally associated with {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|qid=Q17218816|lt=Imperial Mausolea|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}, such as the {{ill|Mausoleum of Emperor Tenji|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum of Emperor Tenji|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Nokubi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nokubi Royal Tomb|qq=}} of Emperors Tenmu and Jitō. Discoveries in eastern Japan, including Inarizuka Kofun, {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} (Gunma Prefecture), and {{ill|Kyozuka Kofun|ja|経塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyozuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 経塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} (Yamanashi Prefecture), have broadened the understanding of octagonal kofun. Among approximately 150,000 kofun nationwide, only 15 examples of octagonal kofun are known.
== Access == From {{ill|Seiseki-sakuragaoka Station|en|Seiseki-sakuragaoka Station|qid=Q1064646|lt=Seiseki-Sakuragaoka Station|1=Seiseki-sakuragaoka Station}} (Keio Line): * '''Sakura 72/73 Bus Routes:''' Board the {{ill|Keio Bus|ja|京王バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keio Bus|1=Keio Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133861256}} bound for {{ill|Tama-Center Station|en|Tama-Center Station|qid=Q988468|lt=Tama Center Station|1=Tama-Center Station}} or Tama Southern Regional Hospital. Alight at "Ochikawa" stop and walk 7 minutes.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Usuizuka Kofun|ja|臼井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usuizuka Kofun|qq=}}: Located 100 meters west of Inarizuka Kofun. Similar construction date and multi-chambered design. * {{ill|Kōshinzuka Kofun|ja|庚申塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshinzuka Kofun|qq=}}: Unexcavated; presumed to be 10 meters in diameter. * {{ill|Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|ja|塚原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukahara Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塚原古墳|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 塚原古墳}}: A group of circular kofun built from the mid-6th to mid-7th century. * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|lt=Nakawada Yokoanabo Cluster|lt=Nakawada Yokoanabo Tomb Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakawada Yokoanabo Tomb Cluster|qq=}}: The largest tomb cluster in Tama Hills, featuring 19 tombs dating from the early 7th to early 8th century.
== Bibliography == * "Tama City Archaeological Report 39: Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Site - Inarizuka Kofun," 1996 * *Tama City History (Archaeological Edition, Volume 1): Ancient and Medieval* * {{cite book|和書|title=Newly Edited Musashi Topographical Records (巻ノ98)|chapter=Kamikoda Village, Tsukahara|publisher=Geographical Bureau of the Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884}}
== External Links == * [https://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.lg.jp/bunkazai/index.html Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site Information] - Tokyo Education Bureau * [http://www.city.tama.lg.jp/0000003429.html Historic Site of Tama City] - Tama City Official Website

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--Inarizuka Kofun--><!--Inarizuka Kofun--><!--Inarizuka Kofun--><!--Inarizuka Kofun--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Inarizuka kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11596855}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] [[Category:Inari faith]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tama]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
